mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 equities and generating positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% when FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.5%, aggregate consumer spending contracts sharply, depressing corporate revenues and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 20-day returns are negative in greater than 50% of co-increasing months during walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals immediate capacity utilization recovery, triggering short-term equity momentum and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no directional bias with a positive frequency below 52% following positive INDPRO revisions, or the signal reverses during liquidity crunch periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 216.0, rising input costs compress corporate profit margins and increase the probability of restrictive policy shifts, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns remain positive in greater than 65% of observations above the threshold, or the negative correlation vanishes after controlling for concurrent FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 60-day returns in greater than 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power with a Rank IC below 0.01 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output, which directly translates to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive bias following positive GDPC1 revisions, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance with a positive frequency under 50% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 95.0, severe capacity underutilization signals imminent corporate earnings downgrades, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of instances below the threshold, or the signal fails to maintain a negative Rank IC greater than -0.03 across rolling evaluation windows.
